items.title: Batch feature normalization in classical ML
items.description: Before training SVMs, logistic regression, or k-NN, pipelines standardize or min-max scale entire feature columns with vectorized `mean`, `std`, and arithmetic on the design matrix — often 10–100× faster than row-wise Python loops on large tabular data.

items.title: Image and signal preprocessing
items.description: Flattened image batches, audio spectrograms, and sensor windows are stored as NumPy arrays. Vectorized clipping, log transforms, and per-channel normalization prepare tensors for CNNs and time-series models without per-pixel Python iteration.

items.title: Synthetic label and weight generation
items.description: Class weights, sample weights, and thresholded pseudo-labels are computed with boolean masks and `np.where` across millions of rows — a vectorized step that sits between feature extraction and model fitting.

items.title: Quick EDA statistics on numeric columns
items.description: Vectorized reductions (`np.nanmean`, `np.percentile`, `np.histogram`) power exploratory summaries on raw numeric blocks extracted from DataFrames, giving fast distributional checks before committing to a heavier pandas groupby pass.
